items.title: 'Bleak motorways' in Canberra's south to gain housing under draft plan

children.content: The plan is for mid-rise housing to be developed closest to transport stops and shopping centres, with high-rise development reserved for the Woden town centre and north Curtin.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: 'I was in tears': Living without a heater through Canberra's winter

children.content: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she is not the only one.

image.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

items.title: New AI-powered alert system to warn drivers of approaching wildlife

children.content: An estimated 10 million animals are killed on Australian roads each year. It's hoped a new artificial intelligence-powered alert system being trialled on the Monaro Highway near Canberra will reduce the toll.

image.altText: A wildlife sign with a digital panel at the bottom.

items.title: 'Her life is ruined': Hellenic Club cleared of failing to record problem gambling

children.content: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

items.title: 'We didn't give up': Afghan women's refugee cricketers celebrate ICC decision

children.content: Five years since fleeing Afghanistan after the Taliban returned to power, the country's women's refugee cricket team has secured a pathway to compete at the international level.

image.altText: Benafsha Hashimi, Afghanistan Women's Cricket Team

items.title: 'I didn't feel safe': Mum reveals concerns before childcare closure

children.content: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

image.altText: A mother stands with a serious expression with her two children.

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

gallery.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

thumbnail.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

items.title: Origin Energy investigating 'potential' customer data breach

children.content: Origin Energy says it does not believe customer credit card or bank details have been affected by a suspected data breach at the company.

gallery.altText: Origin's red and white logo is seen on a pile of company reports

thumbnail.altText: Origin's red and white logo is seen on a pile of company reports

items.title: Minister says Australian IS-linked 'Doctor Jihad' can 'rot' in Iraqi jail

children.content: Perth paediatrician Tareq Kamleh went to Syria to join the Islamic State group in 2015 and has, until now, been presumed dead.

image.altText: Dr Tareq Kamleh

items.title: Actor to appeal after losing defamation case against Rebel Wilson

children.content: Hollywood actor Rebel Wilson successfully defends a defamation case brought against her by the star of her directorial debut.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson outside court April 30 for defamation trial

items.title: Former Socceroos captain Paul Wade reveals probable CTE diagnosis

children.content: Paul Wade says he wants to share his experience to raise awareness about concussion, repeated head impacts and brain health across all levels of football.

image.altText: Paul Wade

items.title: Lewis breaks Australian 100m record, beats five-time Olympic champ

children.content: Torrie Lewis shows she is hitting her straps ahead of the Commonwealth Games by beating her own record mark during a meet in Slovakia.

image.altText: Torrie Lewis unties her shoelaces while sitting on the track

items.title: Why the Houthi threat against Saudi ships 'spells real trouble' for the war

children.content: The Houthi rebels, described as one of Iran's "merchants of chaos", have threatened to widen the conflict in the Middle East by declaring a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. Here's what could happen next.

articles.title: Anxiety as Australia issues new travel advice for Middle East countries

children.content: As conflict between the United States and Iran escalates once again, the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade has issued a warning to "reconsider" travel to several more countries in the region.

image.altText: Woman at airport

articles.title: Hegseth outlines cost of Iran war at Senate hearing

children.content: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

items.title: Jewish community group details 'unprecedented' security needs

children.content: Police engagement failed to engender a sense of safety in Melbourne's Jewish community in the face of rising "vitriol", the antisemitism royal commission has heard.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a blue blazer stands in front of a Royal Commission sign.

items.title: The risks and claims behind these popular wellness treatments

children.content: Wellness and anti-aging clinics are popping up across Australia. But do their treatments work and are they safe?

image.altText: A graphic showing a man lifting weights, a close up of a woman's face and a hand holding a syringe

items.title: Unlawful excavation of river cliff disturbs Aboriginal ancestral remains

children.content: A cliff site in South Australia's Riverland, home to Aboriginal ancestral remains, has been excavated without authorisation.

View of a development site near the river's edge. Houses scattered behind the site.

View of a development site near the river's edge. Houses scattered behind the site.

items.title: Gates Foundation met with Epstein about 30 times, review finds

children.content: Bill Gates was aware of Jeffrey Epstein's 2008 sex offence conviction during their association, an external review of the Gates Foundation finds.

image.altText: Gates walks among a crowd of reports and Capitol Hill staff, giving a small, tight-lipped smile.

items.title: 'Why would I do that?': Boy in court charged with murdering mother, stabbing sister

children.content: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in Perth's south-east, as police say the girl is being treated in hospital for "very serious" stab wounds.

image.altText: Police tape off a suburban driveway

items.title: Six beluga whales make successful journey by air from Canada to US

children.content: Six beluga whales from a defunct marine park in Canada have been successfully moved to new homes in the US.

items.title: Judge says there's a 'real risk' to safety of Bondi Beach attack witnesses

children.content: Twenty witnesses in the Bondi Beach terror attack case have been granted anonymity, with a judge saying there is a "real risk" to their safety.

image.altText: Flowers laid in tribute to bondi victims.

items.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

items.title: Cameron McEvoy has lived a life between trips to Glasgow

children.content: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

items.title: NT enacts 'significant overhaul' of child protection system

children.content: The Northern Territory parliament has passed changes to child protection laws, with the Country Liberal Party describing its amendments as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

items.title: States can use their own emergency warnings when AusAlert goes live

children.content: State and territory representatives had voiced significant concerns about the rollout of the national emergency warning system in a meeting with the minister Kristy McBain.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: 25,000-year-old grass-burning ritual unearthed in Victorian cave

children.content: Evidence of ancient burnt grasses unearthed in a Victorian cave suggest the old ancestors of the GunaiKurnai people used the cave for fire rituals during the Ice Age.

items.title: Mum loses second son in car crash as driver granted bail
